ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for braised chicken neck; no dedicated braised-neck code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for braised chicken neck; A0CKN (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept specifically for braised chicken neck identified as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of braised chicken neck is approximately 100 g, representing one or two neck pieces with bone, as commonly served in Singaporean hawker or home-cooked meals.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, neck, meat only, cooked — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for braised chicken neck (F11010018). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 5.1 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central NDB 05066 (chicken neck, meat only, cooked, simmered) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately; this is documented as an adapted value.

gi_source.title: N/A — Braised Chicken Neck contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Braised chicken neck contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g; glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel placeholder consistent with the template field type; gi_category is set to not_applicable and gi_source documents the not-applicable rationale. No GI value is assigned. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (52 mg/100g); moderate saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Braised chicken neck is low in sodium at 52 mg/100g and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided the braising sauce is prepared with minimal added soy sauce or salt; the saturated fat content (~5.1 g/100g) should be monitored in the context of overall daily saturated fat intake to avoid contributing to arterial stiffness.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Braised chicken neck contains 0.0 g carbohydrate and 0.0 g sugar per 100 g, resulting in negligible glycaemic impact and making it suitable for blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high fat content (18.1 g/100g) may slow gastric emptying and individuals should ensure the braising sauce does not contain significant added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (18.1 g/100g) and estimated saturated fat (~5.1 g/100g); cholesterol 70 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Braised chicken neck has a high total fat content of 18.1 g/100g with an estimated saturated fat of approximately 5.1 g/100g and cholesterol of 70 mg/100g, which may contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, choose lean accompaniments, and request braising sauces with minimal added oil or salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~5.1 g/100g; no omega-3 fatty acids; no dietary fibre
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Braised chicken neck is not recommended as a primary protein source for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its estimated saturated fat content (~5.1 g/100g), which may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6), absence of omega-3 fatty acids, and lack of dietary fibre; pairing with anti-inflammatory sides such as leafy greens or omega-3-rich fish is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised chicken neck is a cooked dark-meat dish prepared from the neck portion of the chicken, including the bone, which is gently braised in a seasoned liquid — typically soy sauce, water, and aromatics such as ginger, garlic, and star anise — until the meat is tender and the collagen-rich connective tissue yields a characteristic gelatinous texture. It is a popular preparation in Singaporean Chinese home cooking and hawker cuisine, often served as a side dish or accompaniment to rice. Per 100 g, it provides 247 kcal, 19.6 g protein, 18.1 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~5.1 g), and 0.0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food with negligible glycaemic impact. Sodium is low at 52 mg/100g when prepared without excessive soy sauce. The gelatinous quality arises from collagen hydrolysis during braising, contributing to a rich mouthfeel. Clinically, it is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to zero carbohydrate content, suitable for hypertension at moderate portions with sodium-controlled braising sauce, but should be consumed in moderation by individuals with cardiovascular disease or rheumatoid arthritis due to its saturated fat content. It is halal-certified when prepared with halal-compliant ingredients.

notes: carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11010018); braised chicken neck is a protein-and-fat food with no carbohydrate content — confirmed zero. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — naturally zero as this is a meat product with no plant-based fibre. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions for braised chicken neck are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database; USDA FoodData Central NDB 05066 provides total fat and saturated fat but not full fatty acid profile for this specific preparation. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data beyond those reported by HPB (potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) not available in the HPB Foodo database for this specific entry as of 2026. Saturated fat value of 5.1 g/100g is an adapted estimate from USDA FoodData Central NDB 05066 (chicken neck, meat only, cooked, simmered), as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately for this item. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g); gi_category set to not_applicable. The glycemic_index field is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value consistent with the RESULT template integer field type; this does not represent a measured GI of zero but indicates the field is not applicable for this negligible-carbohydrate food, as fully documented in gi_source and gi_category.
